Name: Gravity Drop
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Wearing rubber soled shoes for traction (tennis shoes, etc.) stand on a step or stairway as though you were climbing upward. Feet are parallel, and hip-width apart.
With one hand or both, hold onto railing or other object for support.
Edge your feet backward until the heels are off the stairs and your are hanging onto the stair with the balls of your feet.
Make sure feet remain pointed straight ahead. Let the weight of your body drop your heels off the stair.
You will feel a great stretch in your low leg musculature. The key is to keep your hips over your heels and your shoulders in line with them also.
Hold.

Fun Facts

This exercise forces proper loading of all the load joints of the body by physically stacking them one atop the other.

Name: Standing Quad Stretch
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand on one foot and bend the other leg back and place the top of the foot on the back of a chair or a block.
The height of the foot dictates the amount of stretch in the quadriceps muscles(thighs).
Keep your hips and shoulders square to the wall.
Try to tilt your butt under(suck and tuck).
Hold. If needed, hold onto something for balance.

Fun Facts

This exercise stretches the muscles of the hip and repositions the pelvis.

Name: Hero's Pose
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Kneel down with your knees and ankles together and sit on your heels. If this is too difficult, place a pillow between your ankles and sit on that to raise your butt up from the ankle level.
Relax your upper body as your roll your hips forward to place the arch into your low back.
Hold.

Fun Facts

Promotes bilateral pelvic extension.

Name: Standing Arm Circles
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand facing mirror with your feet pointed straight ahead. Place your finger tips into the pad of each hand and point your thumb straight out.
This hand position is imperative to the exercise being done correctly. It is called the "golfer's grip".
Squeeze your shoulder blades together backwards and bring your arms out to your sides at shoulder level.
With your palms facing downward, circle up and forward for the repetitions specified. With your palms facing upward, circle up and back for the desired repetitions.
Remember to keep your feet straight and your shoulder blades squeezed together.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes bilateral lumbar function through thoracic stabilization.

Name: Crocodile
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your legs extended out straight.
Pull your toes back toward your ankles.
Put your right foot on top of the toes of your left foot ( the achilles tendon/heel should sit between the other foot's big toe and second toe).
Extend your arms out to the sides level with your shoulders, palms facing into the floor. If you crossed your left leg over to the right foot then you must keep going in that direction.
Pull the hip off the floor by rolling sideways onto the other hip.
Tighten your thighs and your butt muscles.
Hold.
Turn the head in the opposite direction. Hold this position and breathe; keep the thighs tight and your upper body relaxed.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes knee extension and spine rotation.

Name: Hip Crossover Stretch - Palms Up
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with both knees bent and your feet flat on the floor pointed straight ahead.
Place your arms out to the side at shoulder level, with your palms facing up, backs of hands on the floor.
Cross your left ankle over your right knee and rotate the ankle/knee junction down toward the floor.
Your left foot should now be flat on the floor, along with the outside of your right leg.
Look in the opposite direction and relax your neck and shoulders.
Press the left knee away from your body using the left hip muscles.
Hold as directed on your menu, then switch sides and repeat.

Fun Facts

This e-cise promotes hip and spinal rotation.

Name: Sitting Floor
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Sit against a wall with your legs straight out in front of you.
Your buttocks and upper back should be against the wall the entire time.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together and hold.
Do not lift the shoulders; only squeeze them back and down.
Tighten the thighs and flex the feet back so that your toes are pointing back toward you.
The keys are to keep your blades pulled together, your thighs tight and your feet flexed back.
Hold as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes thoracic extension while limiting the rotation ability of the lower load joints.

Name: Sitting Floor Twist
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Sit on the floor with your legs straight out in front of you.
Bend your right leg and cross it over the left leg.
Place the left elbow on the outside of the right knee while it is crossed over.
Roll your hips forward to create an arch in your low back.
It is very important to hold the arch throughout this e-cise.
Tighten the thigh in your straight leg and pull your toes back toward you.
Now, twist your upper body to the right using your back muscles to rotate your spine.
Turn your head to the right as your twist your back.
Hold this position and breathe.
Hold as directed on your menu and repeat on other side.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes mid thoracic extension through rotation in a hip disparity.

Name: Sitting Femur Rotations
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Sit on the floor with both legs extended straight out in front of you with your feet about a foot apart.
Place your hands about 4 inches behind your hips with fingers pointed away from your hips.
Tighten your thighs and pull your toes back and hold.
Roll your hips forward to place an arch in your lower back.
Initiating the motion from your hips, rotate your legs and feet in and out.
Do not lean too heavily onto your hands.
After 20 repetitions widen your feet about 2 feet apart, after another 20 repetitions widen your feet about 3 feet apart.
Repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes internal and external rotation of the hip while under pelvic / spinal load.

Name: Cats & Dogs
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Start on your hands and knees, where your wrists are placed directly under your shoulders and your knees directly underneath your hips.
CAT: Starting with your hips, tuck your pelvis to round your lower back and spine up towards the ceiling while dropping your head and pulling your shoulder blades away from each other.
DOG: Starting with your hips, roll your pelvis forward to put the arch in your back while collapsing your shoulder blades together and look up toward the ceiling. Be sure not to shrug your shoulders towards your ears.
This exercise promotes bilateral spine flexion and extension while loading the shoulder and hip joints.

Fun Facts

Our spine is a segmented bone made up of 33 vertebrae that need to work as one unit.  This E-cise helps to encourage proper movement and function of our spine as a unit.

Name: Pelvic Tilts
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your knees bent. Make sure your hips, knees and feet are aligned.
Roll your hips backward to flatten your back to the floor and then roll them forward to arch your low back. Remember to relax your upper back.
REPEAT

Fun Facts

This exercise takes the lumbar spine through flexion and extension through the pelvic movement.

Name: Static Back Abdominal Crunches
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your feet on the wall and pointed straight up, knees bent at 90 degrees.
You may also put your legs up over a block or chair.
Place your hands behind your head with fingers interlaced, keep your elbows back.
Look up and back and use your abdominal muscles to pull your upper body up off the floor about 2 inches, then return to floor.
Remember to keep your elbows back.
Do not pull on your neck with your arms/hands; use only your abdominal muscles to generate the movement.
Repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise takes the lumbar spine through flexion and extension through the pelvic movement.

Name: Assisted Hip Lift
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your feet on the wall, knees bent at about a 90 degree angle.
Keep the feet pointing straight up, in line with your knees and hips.
Cross the ankle of one foot to just above the other knee.
If doing this lifts your butt off of the floor, then scoot farther away from the wall.
Press the crossed leg toward the wall using your hip muscles.
Do not allow your hips to shift to the side.
Keep the spine in alignment.
Hold this position as directed on your menu.
Switch sides and repeat.

Fun Facts

Actions such as crossing your legs in a chair should be easily done on both sides of your body. This E-cise helps maintain this ability.

Name: Hooklying Knee Pillow Squeezes
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your knees bent and your feet flat on the floor pointed straight ahead.
Place a towel under your lower back if instructed to do so on your menu.
Put a pillow between your knees.
Place arms out to sides at a 45 degree angle with palms up.
Squeeze your knees inward into the pillow then release.
Repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

Promotes bilateral pelvic stabilization through the adductors.

Name: Airbench
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand with your back against a wall with feet and knees hip width apart and feet pointed straight.
Walk your feet away from the wall while sliding your body down at the same time.
You will be "seated" in an invisible chair, with your knees bent to 105 degrees.
Your hips are just slightly higher than your knees; your ankles are slightly ahead of your knees.
Your lower back should be completely flat against the wall.
Your arms can hang down to your sides, or rest your hands gently on your lap.
Hold as directed on your menu.
Keep the weight in your heels, do not press forward on your toes.
DO NOT DO THIS E-CISE IN SOCKS!
DO THIS EXERCISE IN ATHLETIC SHOES OR ON A YOGA MAT!

Fun Facts

A key component in walking is Quad strength. In this E-cise we are increasing the strength of this muscle group.

Name: Hooklying Gluteal Contractions
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your knees bent and your feet pointed straight ahead.
Lie with your arms out at 45 degrees and with your palms up.
Begin squeezing and releasing your buttocks muscles without contracting your stomach/abdominal muscles.

Fun Facts

Promotes hip stabilization through engagement of the hip extensors.

Name: Static Wall
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your legs pointed straight up and against the wall, upper body relaxed with arms out to sides at about 45 degrees and palms facing up feet should be hip width apart.
When getting into this position, get your hips as close as possible to the wall, so once you are in position your butt is also as close as possible to the wall.
If your hips are lifting off the floor then scoot your butt back until you are able to place your hips flat. As you get more functional, your hips will sit closer to the wall and be flat on the floor at the same time.
In this position tighten your thighs and pull ALL of your toes down and back evenly towards your face.
Your feet must be pointed straight(parallel) for your hips to properly do the needed work to stabilize your spine.
Hold as directed on your menu.
This exercise promotes thoracic extension while limiting the rotation ability of the lower load joints.

Fun Facts

Promotes hip stabilization through engagement of the hip extensors.

Name: Static Back
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your legs up over your inflatable large block or up over a chair/couch.
Your arms are at 45 degrees and your palms up.
Try to relax your upper back and notice if your low back is flat evenly from left to right.
HOLD

Fun Facts

This exercise creates horizontal load between shoulder and pelvis, which contributes to thoracic extension by engaging the stabilizers and flexors of the hip. This position helps prevent compensation from occurring while performing other types of muscular work.

Name: Supine Groin Stretch
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with one leg up over your inflatable large block or chair.
Your other leg is straight out on the floor with it propped up from the side.
Your arms should be at 45 degrees with your palms up. Relax your upper body and notice that your low back should begin to get flatter to the floor the longer you are in this position.
HOLD and relax, then repeat on other leg.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes an elongation of the hip and groin muscles. The effect of this action will create a chain reaction of muscular response in the upper body.

Name: Gluteal Extensions
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your side with the leg that is on the floor bent back at a 90 degree angle at the knee.
Prop your head up with the arm that is on floor, putting your elbow straight up from the shoulder.
You can use your other hand to keep yourself stable.
Tighten the thigh of the upper leg and flex your toes back toward your head.
Raise the upper leg into the air and point the toes of the raised leg toward the floor while the heel points up toward the ceiling.
Your foot should be at the same level as your head.
Bend the knee of the raised leg slightly, then kick your leg straight.
Keep the leg elevated throughout the e-cise.
Repeat as directed on your menu, then switch sides and repeat.

Fun Facts

The exercise isolates the muscle of the lateral hip that perform the function of propelling the body at different phases of the walking motion.

Name: Supine Hamstring Rolls
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your knees bent, arms out to your sides at 45 degrees with palms facing up
Feet should be pointed straight and placed upon a 6 inch foam roll or other similar cylindrical object
Bridge your buttocks/hips up off the floor and hold them up in the air as you roll your feet back and forth (from heel to toes) on the roll
Be sure to keep your butt/hips elevated; do not let them drop
REPEAT as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes proper hamstring function on a loaded pelvis.

Name: Supine Leg Raises
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with one leg bent and the other leg extended straight out in front of you.
Prop yourself up on your elbows and look straight ahead.
Tighten the thigh of your straight leg and pull your toes backtoward you.
Hold this tightened position while lifting and lowering this leg from the floor up to the level of the bent leg and down again.
Repeat as directed on your menu.
Switch legs and repeat.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes pelvic stabilization and knee extension.

Name: Prone Ankle Squeezes
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie face down on the floor resting your forehead on your hands.
Your elbows should be out to the side.
Place a pillow between your ankles/feet.
Curl the pillow up to your buttocks and hold with 90 degrees bend in your knees.
Squeeze the pillow with your ankles/feet and release.
Repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise works the pelvic stabilizers using external rotators of the femur.

Name: Inner/Outer Thighs
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your side.
Rest your head on your hand and brace yourself with the other arm, placing your palm flat on the floor.
Bend the top leg and place your foot flat on the floor in front of your thigh.
Raise and lower the bottom leg up 3-4 inches off the floor then back to the floor.
Keep the thigh of the bottom leg tight and your toes flexed back.
Switch sides by stacking the legs, then bend the knee of the bottom leg back behind you.
Be sure your knees are stacked on top of each other.
Hold the top leg straight, with your thigh tight and your foot flexed.
Raise and lower the top leg for the number of repetitions given on your menu.
Repeat as directed on your menu.
Roll to the other side and repeat the entire sequence.

Fun Facts

Remember Jane Fonda and her crazy 80�s workouts? Well, turns out she wasn�t so crazy. This E-cise helps proper knee position and hip movement all while working the muscles of the leg and low back.  If it�s harder for you to do on one side vs. the other, this is asymmetry showing up in your body.

Name: Cobra
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your stomach, propped up on your hands, toes together and heels relaxed/dropped out to sides.
Let your shoulder blades collapse together and keep your elbows extended.
Be sure to relax your buttocks muscles so that your heels drop out to sides.
Hold this position as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes proper lumbar extension through thoracic extension.

Name: Runners Stretch
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Kneel on the floor and place the back of your left heel to the front of the right knee
Be sure that you are up on the toes of your right foot, with bottom of the foot pointing behind you
Keep the left foot, right knee and right foot in line with each other
Stand up and bend over while rolling your hips back to place an arch into your lower back
The heel of your right foot should now be on the floor
Tighten your thighs while relaxing your upper body
Keep your weight on the inside of each foot and keep your lower back arched
Hold as directed on your menu
Switch sides and repeat

Fun Facts

This exercise is designed to isolate the hamstrings under bilateral pelvic demand.

Name: Wall Stork
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand with your back against the wall with your feet pointed straight and hip width apart.
Your heels, hips, upper back and head should be against the wall.
Place one foot upon a chair in front of you.
Your knee should be bent to 90 degrees, thigh parallel to floor.
Make sure that your foot is pointed straight.
Do not allow the down leg to bend.
Hold this position as directed on your menu.
Do not allow the leg/hip of the down leg to shift out to the side.
Switch legs and repeat.

Fun Facts

This exercise reseats the hip and re-educates the lumbar spine to stabilize.

Name: ABduction-ADduction w/ Feet Close
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your knees bent at a 90 degree angle and your feet flat on the wall.
Keep your feet parallel, spaced about 3 inches apart, and pointed to the ceiling.
Keep your upper body relaxed, with your palms up and your arms flat on the floor at about a 45 degree angle from your body.
Bring your knees together slowly until they touch.
Bring your knees apart slowly so that your feet roll out to the sides.
The bottoms of your feet will come off of the wall and the outsides of your feet will touch the wall.
Return your knees to the start position to complete one repetition of the e-cise.
Repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

Femur rotation is crucial for everyday movements such as sitting, getting in and out of a car, walking and running. This E-cise helps promote femur rotation at the hip joint and has specific emphasis on outward rotation.

Name: Wall Drop
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand on a slantboard (or a step-ladder will do), feet pointed straight and hip width apart with your head, shoulders and butt against the wall.
Let your arms hang down at your sides.
Relax your stomach and upper body.
Do not allow your knees to bend.
You will feel a stretch in your calf/lower leg muscles.
Hold as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

Just about everyone can use a good stretch on the calf muscles.  This E-cise not only stretches the calf muscles but forces the body to stay in perfect alignment while doing it, which in turn helps to align our spine correctly.

Name: Static Extension Position
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Start on your hands and knees.
Move your hands about 6 inches forward and then move your upper body forward so that your shoulders are above your hands.
Your hips are now forward of your knees about 6 inches.
Keep your elbows straight and allow your shoulder blades to collapse together while your low back arches.
Drop your head.
Your low back arches because your hips roll forward to allow this movement to occur.
HOLD

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes lumbar and thoracic extension through bilateral hip demand.

Name: Cobra on Elbows
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lying on your stomach propped up on your elbows. Allow your heels to drop out to the side with your toes together.
Pull your hands away from each other, pivoting on your elbows. Allow your shoulder blades to collapse together as you continue to pull your hands apart.
HOLD this position and remember to relax your buttocks muscles.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes proper lumbar extension through thoracic extension.

Name: Assisted Hip Lift Position w/ Rocking Chair
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your knees bent.
Arms out to your sides at 45 degrees, palms up.
Cross the ankle of one foot over to just above the knee of the other leg.
Keep the foot that is still on the ground pointed straight ahead.
Gently press the knee of the raised leg away from you and hold.
Use your hip muscles to press the knee forward.
Do not let the hips shift, maintain the external rotation of the thigh.
Roll the foot that is still on the floor forward onto the ball of the foot (raising the heel off the floor), then back to the heel (raising the toes off the floor).
Repeat as directed on your menu.
Switch legs and repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes transferring the work of supporting the body from the primary hip flexors and erector muscles to the muscles of the legs.

Name: Standing Abductor Presses
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand in a doorway or sideways against a wall with your feet pointed straight and hip width apart.
Your arms should be hanging down at your sides.
Your inside foot/ankle should be placed firmly against the doorjamb or wall; this is the "working foot".
Now press sideways against the wall/jamb and then release.
You will feel work in the opposite hip during this e-cise.
Keep your upper body straight but remember to let your stomach relax throughout the entire exercise.
Repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes bilateral hip demand...one hip in an action demand, the other in a stability demand.

Name: Frog Arm Glides
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back
If directed to do so by your menu, place a towel under your lower back
Place the soles of your feet together
Center your feet along the mid-line of your body and let your knees relax down to the sides
Place your hands out from your shoulders with a 90 degree angle at your elbows
Glide both of your arms and hands along the floor so that your hands meet above your head
Return them to the beginning position and repeat as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes scapular rotation while in lumbar extension.

Name: Kneeling Counter Stretch - Modified
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Kneel down and place your forearms onto a chair or block
Back your knees away from the chair, allowing your upper body to drop through your arms
Before you drop your head between your arms, your hips should be ahead of your knees
Allow your spine to slowly drop to an arched position
You should feel tightness between your shoulder blades
Hold as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

Promotes thoracic extension under a pelvic load

Name: Active Bridges w/ Strap
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your knees bent and your feet, hips and shoulders in line.
Your head, neck, shoulders and upper body should be relaxed at all times, keep your arms at 45 deg with palms up.
Place a strap around your legs just above the knees and keep light outward pressure on the strap throughout the e-cise.
Lift your hips off the floor as high as you can and slowly lower them back down.
Keep the motion as smooth and continuous as possible.
Repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes proper bilateral extension of pelvis and spine.

Name: Function Run
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Initiate your running by pulling your knee up in front of you toward your chest
Do not kick your leg up toward your buttocks
As your foot descends to the ground, your heel should hit first
Roll from your heel to the ball of your foot and then to your toes
Your pace should be very slow (treadmill speed would be approx. 2.0 - 2.5 mph)
Your torso should be upright, shoulders relaxed, abdominals relaxed and breathing
Keep your lower back arched
Continue as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

This form of running trains the body in the proper mechanics without allowing room for compensation movements.

Name: Static Back Pull-over Presses w/ Strap
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your legs up over a large block or chair, knees bent at 90 degrees
There should be a small pillow or block placed directly above your head at arms length
Place a strap around your thighs just above the knees at hip width and apply constant pressure outward against the strap throughout the e-cise
Relax your legs, lower back and stomach
Reach arms straight up above your chest, elbows locked and hands clasped together
Now lower your hands down to the block above your head
Press into the block, hold for the indicated time and then release
Do not contract your abdominal muscles, keep the stomach and lower back relaxed
Do not let your arms bend at the elbow, keep them straight throughout the e-cise
Repeat as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

This exercise engages spinal extensors. This Static Back position creates horizontal load between shoulder and pelvis, which contributes to thoracic extension by engaging the stabilizers and flexors of the hip. This position helps prevent compensation from occurring while performing other types of muscular work.

Name: Static Back Pull-Overs w/ Strap
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your legs up over a large block or chair, knees bent at 90 degrees
Place a strap around your thighs just above the knees at hip width and apply constant pressure outward against the strap throughout the e-cise
Relax your legs, lower back and stomach
Reach arms straight up above your chest, elbows locked and hands clasped together
Now lower your hands down to the floor above your head
Do not contract your abdominal muscles, keep the stomach and lower back relaxed
Do not let your arms bend at the elbow, keep them straight throughout the e-cise
If you are unable to lower your hands all the way to the floor behind you, go only as low as you are able while maintaining the straight arm position
Return to the starting position and repeat as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

This exercise re- establishes proper glide of the scapula to promote thoracic extension. This Static Back position creates horizontal load between shoulder and pelvis, which contributes to thoracic extension by engaging the stabilizers and flexors of the hip. This position helps prevent compensation from occurring while performing other types of muscular work

Name: Standing at Wall w/ Pillow
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand at a wall with your heels, hips, upper back and head against the wall
Place a small block or pillow between your knees
Apply gentle pressure, just enough to hold the pillow in place; do not push into it
If you cannot get your head against the wall without straining then hold it in a relaxed position; do not force it
Relax your stomach and your arms and allow your body to adjust to this new position
Remember to keep your feet pointed straight ahead and your stomach relaxed throughout the e-cise
Hold as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes proper positioning of all load joints with the wall as the map.

Name: Standing at Wall
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand at a wall with your heels, hips, upper back and head against the wall
If you cannot get your head against the wall without straining then place it in a relaxed position; do not force it
Relax your stomach and your arms and allow your body to adjust to this new position
Remember to keep your feet pointed straight ahead and your stomach relaxed throughout the e-cise
Hold as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes proper positioning of all load joints with the wall as the map.

Name: Sitting Assisted Hip Lift
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Sit in the middle of a chair with your feet pointed straight ahead, 4-6 inches apart.
Roll your pelvis forward to place a small arch in your low back and hold this position throughout the e-cise.
Cross one foot over the opposite knee.
Using the hip muscles, press down the knee of the crossed leg toward the floor and hold.
You should feel this stretch the lateral hip of the crossed leg and some in your low back from holding the arched position.
Switch legs and repeat.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes opposite hip stabilization through rotation of the femur.

Name: Cross-Crawling
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with both of your legs stretched out and your arms resting beside your body(palms down).
Simultaneously, raise one arm and the opposite leg. The arm remains locked straight as you take it overhead and the opposite leg is bent at the knee joint as you pull that knee up toward your chest.
After you have lifted them up, return them back to the floor and switch sides.
REPEAT

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes proper flexion of both hip and shoulder simultaneously.

Name: Frog Pull-Overs
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your knees bent.
Place the bottoms of your feet together and allow your knees to fall out to the sides(frog position).
Interlace your fingers together and place your hands above your chest with your elbows locked out straight.
Take your hands toward the floor above your head and then return them to above your chest. The key is to not bend your elbows and if your back arches when your hands are headed toward the floor, then that is as far as you allow your hands to drop.
REPEAT

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes proper scapular rotation while in lumbar extension.

Name: Standing Overhead Extension Forward Bend
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand with your feet pointing straight and hip width apart.
Interlace your fingers together and reach your arms overhead, pressing your hands to the ceiling with palms up.
Look up toward your hands and keep your arms straight, do not bend at the elbow.
While in this position roll your hips forward to place an arch in your low back.
Bend forward allowing the hips to dictate how far over you bend.
Be sure to maintain the arch in your low back and keep your elbows straight.
Hold as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes proper positioning of the shoulder over the hip while engaging all of the extensor muscles of the spine and the primary hip flexors.

Name: Supermans
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your stomach with your arms outstretched overhead
Simultaneously lift your arms/hands and legs/feet up about six inches off the floor
It should feel as if you are reaching and lifting in both directions (forward with the arms, backward with the legs)
Hold as directed

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes engagement of the extensors of the trunk, pelvis, and legs

Name: Static Wall Pull-over Presses
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your legs pointed straight up and against the wall, upper body relaxed with arms out to sides at about 45 degrees and palms facing up
When getting into this position, get your hips as close as possible to the wall, so once you are in position your butt is also as close as possible to the wall
If your hips are lifting off the floor then scoot your butt back until you are able to place your hips flat. As you get more functional, your hips will sit closer to the wall and be flat on the floor at the same time
In this position tighten your thighs and pull your toes down and back toward you
Your feet must be pointed straight for your hips to properly do the needed work to stabilize your spine
There should be a small pillow or block placed directly above your head at arms length
Reach arms straight up above your chest, elbows locked and hands clasped together
Now lower your hands down to the block above your head
Press into the block, hold for the indicated time and then release
Do not contract your abdominal muscles, keep the stomach and lower back relaxed
Do not let your arms bend at the elbow, keep them straight throughout the e-cise
Bring your arms back up above your chest after each set of ten
Repeat as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes bilateral hip and lumbar flexion to re-establish the muscular chain between the trunk and pelvis with an emphasis on increased activity of the spinal extensors.

Name: Flexion Abdominals
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your feet on the wall.
Your hips should be 4 to 6 inches from the wall.
Your feet should be parallel and pointed straight up.
Both your feet and knees should be kept hip width apart throughout the e-cise.
Interlace your fingers together behind your head.Interlace your fingers together behind your head.
Keep your elbows back, pointed out to the sides at all times.
Look up and behind you at the ceiling.
Use your abdominal muscles to lift your chest and shoulder blades about 2 inches off the floor.
Repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise encourages proper function of the hip flexors and lumbar flexors by taking the hip joint to the extreme range of flexion.

Name: Active Cobra Wishbone
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your stomach propping your head and chest up on your elbows
Hold your head up, looking straight ahead
Bring your knees together and keep them together throughout the e-cise
Pull your hands apart so your shoulders relax and your hands are outside of your elbows
Your thumbs should be straight and pointing toward the ceiling (hands closed in loose fists)
Your forearms should be in contact with ground
Your elbows should be shoulder width or just wider than shoulder width apart
Flex your feet and point your toes to the floor
Pull your feet apart and sideways to create a wishbone shape with your feet and knees
Hold this position as instructed on your menu

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes forced internal rotation of femur on an extended pelvis and spine.

Name: Wall Towels
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand at a wall with your feet pointed straight and hip width apart.
Your heels should be about 3 inches from the wall.
Your head, upper back and butt should be against the wall.
Place a roll behind your low back and another behind your neck.
Hold as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

The towels remind our spine of its natural "S" curve that is lost when we sit for long periods during the day.

Name: Standing Abductor Presses w/ Strap
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand with your feet pointed straight and hip-width apart.
Place a strap around your thighs just above the knees.
Your arms should be hanging down at your sides.
Without bending your knees, place light pressure outward against the strap (as if trying to spread your knees apart but the strap will not allow them to); then release.
Do not try to press too hard outward.
Repeat as directed on your menu.
Let your stomach relax throughout the entire exercise

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes bilateral lumbar demand through lateral hip stabilization.

Name: Assisted Hip Lift w/ Rocking Chair
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your knees bent.
Arms out to your sides at 45 degrees, palms up.
Cross the ankle of one foot over to just above the knee of the other leg.
Keep the foot that is still on the ground pointed straight ahead.
Gently press the knee of the raised leg away from you and hold.
Use your hip muscles to press the knee forward.
Do not let the hips shift, maintain the external rotation of the thigh.
Roll the foot that is still on the floor forward onto the ball of the foot (raising the heel off the floor), then back to the heel (raising the toes off the floor).
Repeat as directed on your menu.
Switch legs and repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes transferring the work of supporting the body from the primary hip flexors and erector muscles to the muscles of the legs.

Name: MFB - Wishbone
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your stomach in the Modified Floor Block position
Your feet should be pigeon-toed and your buttocks relaxed
Elevate your bent arms 4 to 9 inches by placing your forearms and elbows on blocks
Bend both knees to a 90 degrees angle and flex your toes down toward the floor.
Holding that position with your inner thighs touching, allow your ankle to drop away from each other in a slow controlled manner.
Once they have reach the end of their range of motion maintain the 90 degree angle at your knee for the duration specified on your menu.
Reverse the steps to come out of the exercise.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes pelvic and lumbar extension by way of the rotation of the femur (thigh bone).

Name: Counter Stretch - Modified
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand facing a wall or stable doorway.
Any stable object can be used if it is about chest high.
Cross your arms with one forearm stacked on top of the other and place them about chest high on the wall or object.
Keep your feet pointed straight ahead.
Walk your feet back so that your weight is in your toes.
Keep the hips, knees and feet in alignment.
Kick your buttocks/hips back to place an arch in your lower back and tighten your quads.
Hold as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

Sitting at a desk causes our spine to round and our knees to flex. This E-cise is helpful in lengthening the whole back line of the body.

Name: Standing Hamstring Curls
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand facing and holding onto a doorjamb or pillar for support. Your feet should be pointing straight and hip width apart.
Tilt your hips under to flatten the low back and hold this position throughout the entire e-cise.
Lift one foot off the floor and try to touch your buttocks by using your hamstring muscles and then return it to the floor.
Relax your upper body.
Do not allow the hip on the support leg to jut out sideways.
Repeat as directed on your menu and then switch legs.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes equal loading of the pelvis and lumbar extension.

Name: Core Abdominals
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Position 1-Lie on your stomach propped up on your elbows, your elbows should be directly under your shoulders.
Tuck your toes under and bridge your body off the floor.
Without moving your elbows, pull your hands apart and let your shoulder blades collapse together.
Position 2-3- Lie on your side propped up on your elbow with the other arm resting along your body and your feet stacked on top of each other.
Lift your hips up off the floor and let your shoulder blades come together.
Position 4-Lie on your back propped up on your elbows.
Flex your feet so you toes point to the ceiling and contract  your thighs.
Lift your hips/buttocks up off the floor keeping your toes pulled back and feet straight.
As you lift your hips off the floor, try to think about pushing your chest to the ceiling and allowing your shoulder blades to come together.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes load joint stabilization in a horizontal plane.

Name: Hand-Leg Opposite Blocked - Pressdowns
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your stomach with one arm and the opposite leg propped up on 6 inch blocks/pillows
Your forehead should be down on the floor
The arm and leg that are not on blocks should be extended
The block supporting your leg should be placed under the knee
The block supporting your arm should be placed between your elbow and wrist
Bend the knee of the propped up leg and press that knee down into the pillow and release
The knee should be bent to a 90 degree angle
The foot of the bent knee should be flexed
Repeat as directed on your menu, then switch sides and repeat

Fun Facts

Promotes alternate shoulder and hip stabilization

Name: Static Wall Pull-Backs
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your legs pointed straight up and against the wall, upper body relaxed with arms out to sides at about 45 degrees and palms facing up.
When getting into this position, get your hips as close as possible to the wall, so once you are in position your butt is also as close as possible to the wall.
If your hips are lifting off the floor then scoot your butt back until you are able to place your hips flat. As you get more functional, your hips will sit closer to the wall and be flat on the floor at the same time.
In this position tighten your thighs and pull your toes down and back toward your hips.
Your feet must be pointed straight for your hips to properly do the needed work to stabilize your spine.
Pull the heel of one leg off the wall 2 to 4 inches and return it to the wall.
The heel is moved off the wall with the front hip muscles doing the work.
The key is not to use your upper body for help and to also NOT push the other leg into the wall for leverage.
Switch legs and repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes bilateral hip and lumbar flexion to re-establish the muscular chain between the trunk and pelvis.

Name: Standing Knee Pillow Squeezes
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand with your feet pointing straight and hip width apart.
Place a pillow between your knees and using your inner thighs, squeeze and release the pillow evenly.
Do not use your abdominal muscles or upper body; both should remain relaxed throughout the e-cise.
Repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes stabilization of the pelvis with proper position of all the load joints.

Name: Hooklying Isolated Hip Flexor Lifts
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your knees bent and your feet flat on the floor pointed straight ahead.
Place a towel under your lower back if instructed to do so on your menu.
Place arms out to sides at a 45 degree angle with palms up.
Keep your upper body relaxed throughout the e-cise.
Lift the heel off the floor on the non moving leg thoughout the set.
Lift the opposite foot 3 to 6 inches off the floor and then return it back to the floor.
Keep the knee of the foot you are lifting in line with the shoulder and the foot in line with the knee.
Switch legs and continue.
Repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

Promotes unilateral hip flexor engagement for both hip flexion and stabilization.

Name: Sitting Isolated Hip Flexor Lifts
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Sit in the middle of a chair with your feet pointed straight ahead, 4-6 inches apart.
Let your arms hang down at your sides.
Roll your hips forward to place an arch in your low back.
Lift one foot 4 to 6 inches off the floor and then lower; repeat as directed on your menu.
Use your front hip muscles to generate the movement.
Maintain the arch in your back, keeping your hips rolled forward throughout the e-cise.
It may be beneficial to sit facing a mirror to observe if your upper body is offsetting away from the working leg/hip--do not let this happen.
Switch sides and repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes proper function of the lumbar erectors for stabilization through unilateral hip demand.

Name: Sitting Heel Raises w/ Strap
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Sit in the middle of a chair with your feet pointed straight ahead, 4-6 inches apart.
Place a strap around your knees and apply constant pressure outward against the strap.
Roll your hips forward to place an arch in your low back and hold throughout the exercise.
Lift your heels off the floor using your front hip muscles to generate the movement and then lower them back to the floor.
Repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

The exercise promotes bilateral primary hip flexion demand to transfer workload from lumbar spine to muscle of the hip and pelvis.

Name: Sitting Heel Raises w/ Pillow
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Sit in the middle of a chair with your feet pointed straight ahead, 4-6 inches apart.
Place a pillow between your knees and apply gentle pressure inward, just enough to hold the pillow in place.
Roll your hips forward to place an arch in your low back and hold throughout the exercise.
Lift your heels off the floor using your front hip muscles to generate the movement and then lower them back to the floor.
Repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

The exercise promotes bilateral primary hip flexion demand to transfer workload from lumbar spine to muscle of the hip and pelvis.

Name: Supine Gluteal Contractions
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your knees bent and your feet pointed straight ahead, arms out at 45 degrees with palms up
Your upper body should be relaxed
Squeeze and release your buttocks muscles without contracting your stomach/abdominal muscles
Repeat as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

Promotes hip stabilization through engagement of the hip extensors

Name: Decline Airbench
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand with your back against a wall and your feet parallel and pointing straight ahead
Your hips, upper back and head should be flat against the wall
Walk your feet 1.5 to 2 feet away from the wall
Bend your knees and slide your back down the wall
Slide down until your hips are 2 to 4 inches below your knees
Interlace your fingers together behind your head with your elbows pulled back against the wall
Put pressure in your heels and lift your toes up off the floor
At this point your lower back should be pushed back into the wall
Hold this position as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes pelvic stabilization along with proper function of the primary and secondary hip flexor muscle groups.

Name: Sitting Elbow Curls w/ Pillow
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Sit on the edge of a chair with your feet pointed straight ahead
Roll your hips forward to place an arch in your low back
Place a block or pillow between your knees and press gently with just enough pressure to hold the pillow in place
Place your hands into the golfer's grip on your temples
Hold this starting position; then pull your elbows away from each other to force your upper back muscles to create the work
Do not lose your hip position; keep them rolled forward and maintain the arch in your low back
Pull your elbows back and them close them together in front of your face
Keep your elbows up at shoulder level, do not let them drop down
Repeat as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes bilateral pelvic extension and proper function between the upper and lower body

Name: Sitting Elbow Curls w/ Strap
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Sit in the middle of a chair with your feet pointed straight ahead, 4-6 inches apart.
Roll your hips forward to place an arch in your low back.
Place a strap around your knees and apply constant pressure outward against the strap throughout the e-cise.
Place your hands into the golfer's grip on your temples.
Do not lose your hip position; keep them rolled forward and maintain the arch in your low back.
Pull your elbows back and then close them together in front of your face.
Keep your elbows up at shoulder level, do not let them drop down.
Repeat as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

This exercise is the perfect warm-up for the chicken dance at your next wedding.

Name: Static Back Abductor Presses
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your legs up over a block or chair.
Place a strap around your legs just above the knees.
Place your arms out to the sides at 45 degrees from your body with palms up.
Relax your stomach and upper back.
Try to pull your knees apart by applying pressure outward against the strap, then release.
Repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes hip stabilization bilaterally. This Static Back position creates horizontal load between shoulder and pelvis, which contributes to thoracic extension by engaging the stabilizers and flexors of the hip. This position helps prevent compensation from occurring while performing other types of muscular work.

Name: Static Back Elbow Abduction
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your legs up over a large block or chair, knees bent at 90 degrees.
Relax your legs, back and stomach.
Place a strap around your arms just above your elbows.
Place your knuckles on your temples with your hands in the golfer's grip position so that your elbows are pointed straight up toward the ceiling.
Press out sideways against the strap, then release.
Repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes stability at the shoulder girdle to encourage thoracic extension. This Static Back position creates horizontal load between shoulder and pelvis, which contributes to thoracic extension by engaging the stabilizers and flexors of the hip. This position helps prevent compensation from occurring while performing other types of muscular work.

Name: Supine Double Switch-No Lift
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with both legs straight and flat on the floor, arms at a 45 degree angle to body, palms up.
Place a pillow or small block between your knees and strap around your ankles.
Flex your toes back toward your hips bending at the ankle joint.
Push inward on the pillow and pull outward on the strap simultaneously.
If you find it difficult to do them at the same time, squeeze in on the pillow first and hold, then try to push out on the strap, release both at the same time..
Keep your upper body relaxed.
Repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes stabilization of the hip joint.

Name: Supine Double Double-No Lift
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with both legs straight and flat on the floor, arms at a 45 degree angle to body, palms up.
Place strap around your knees and a pillow or small block between your feet and ankles.
Flex your toes back toward your hips bending at the ankle joint.
Push outward on the strap and squeeze inward on the pillow/block simultaneously.
If you find it difficult to do them at the same time, pull out on the strap first and hold, then try to squeeze in on the pillow, release both at the same time.
Keep your upper body relaxed.
Repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes stabilization of the hip joint.

Name: Supine Femur Rotations
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your knees bent, arms out to sides at 45 degrees with palms facing up.
Straighten one leg out while keeping the other leg bent.
On the straight leg, tighten your thigh and pull your toes back and HOLD.
Rotate your leg and foot in and out with the hip muscles initiating the movement.
DO NOT rotate just the foot - the entire leg should be moving in and out while keeping the thigh tight and toes pulled back.
Be sure to relax your upper body.
Switch legs and repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes stabilization of the hip, pelvis, and lumbar spine.

Name: In-Line Gluteal Contractions
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand with your feet in-line with each other so that the toes of the foot behind are touching the heel of the foot in front.
If your menu directs you to do so, stand with 2-3 inches between the toes of your back foot and heel of your front foot.
It is very important that your feet remain pointed straight ahead through out the e-cise.
In this position, squeeze and release your buttocks muscles, keeping your thighs and stomach muscles relaxed.
Repeat as directed on your menu.
Switch sides and repeat.

Fun Facts

Promotes lateral hip stability in a vertically loaded position.

Name: Static Wall w/ Splits
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your legs pointed straight up and against the wall, upper body relaxed with arms out to sides at about 45 degrees and palms facing up.
When getting into this position, get your hips as close as possible to the wall, so once you are in position your butt is also as close as possible to the wall.
If your hips are lifting off the floor then scoot your butt back until you are able to place your hips flat. As you get more functional, your hips will sit closer to the wall and be flat on the floor at the same time.
Split your legs, allowing them to drop down to the sides, as far as you can without causing pain.
You will feel your inner thighs pulling/stretching.
In this position tighten your thighs and pull your toes down and back toward you.
Your feet must be pointed straight for your hips to properly do the needed work to stabilize your spine.
Hold as directed on your menu.
Be sure to keep your toes pulled back and thighs tight.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes bilateral hip and lumbar flexion to re-establish the muscular chain between the trunk and pelvis with an emphasis on pelvic stability and erector engagement.

Name: Floor/Knee Block
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your stomach with your forehead and nose flat on the floor
Place your knees and elbows onto 6 inch inflatable blocks or stacks of books
Your arms should be above your head, straight out from your shoulders
Your feet should be in the air and relaxed
If 6 inches is too much demand, lower the height of the blocks to allow for a more comfortable position
Relax your lower body and stomach so that you feel the front of your hips falling toward the floor
Lock your elbows and point your thumbs toward each other in the golfer's grip
Your fingers should be curled in so they just touch your palms
Rotate your thumbs to point at the ceiling with the movement coming from your shoulders
Hold this position as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

This exercise forces extension of the thoracic back and pelvis.

Name: Wishbone
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your stomach with your forehead resting on your clasped hands.
Bend your knees to bring your heels up toward your buttocks/hips.
Pull your toes downward toward the floor with the front ankle muscles.
Pull your ankles/heels away from each other, keeping your knees together.
Remember to keep your upper back and shoulders relaxed throughout the e-cise.
Hold as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

Proper gait mechanics requires that our femurs can rotate internally and externally.

Name: Wall Towels Knee Pillow Squeezes
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand at a wall with your feet pointed straight and hip width apart
Your heels should be about 3 inches from the wall
Your head, upper back and butt should be against the wall
Place a roll behind your low back and another behind your neck
Place a small block or pillow between your knees
Press your inner knees/thighs into the pillow and then release
Do not bend your knees
Remember to keep your upper back/shioulders and stomach muscles relaxed throughout the e-cise
Repeat as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes the four load joint design of the body while educating the spine to its proper curve

Name: Wall Towels Gluteal Contractions
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand at a wall with your feet pointed straight and hip width apart
Your heels should be about 3 inches from the wall
Your head, upper back and butt should be against the wall
Place a roll behind your low back and another behind your neck
Squeeze and release your buttocks muscles
Do not squeeze so hard as to cause your hips to come off the wall
Remember to keep your shoulders and upper back relaxed
Repeat as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes the four load joint design of the body while educating the spine to its proper curve

Name: Wall Towels Scapular Contractions
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand at a wall with your feet pointed straight and hip width apart
Your heels should be about 3 inches from the wall
Your head, upper back and butt should be against the wall
Place a roll behind your low back and another behind your neck
Squeeze together and then release your shoulder blades
Repeat as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes the four load joint design of the body while educating the spine to its proper curve

Name: Triangle
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand with your back to a wall.
Your heels should be up against the wall and your feet pointed straight ahead.
Now turn one foot outward and take a 2-3 foot step sideways so that foot ends up parallel to and about 3 inches out from the wall.
Lift your arms so they are straight out from your shoulders, keeping them against the wall.
Your palms should be facing out (backs of hands against the wall) and fingers spread.
Rotate your hips so that both buttocks are against the wall.
Now slide your hips sideways over the foot that did not move and then slide your upper body and arms the opposite direction.
Bend sideways from the waist so that one hand/arm comes down to the parallel foot and the other hand/arm goes straight up the wall.
Look up, tighten your thighs and HOLD.
Be sure that your hips stay against the wall.
Hold as directed on your menu.
Switch sides and repeat.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes proper unilateral hip function.

Name: Lying Supine w/ Pillow
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with both legs straight and flat on the floor.
Place a pillow or small block between your ankles/feet.
Flex your toes back toward your hips bending at the ankle joint.
Try to keep your upper body relaxed, with arms at a 45 degree angle to body, palms up.
Keep your thighs relaxed.
Hold as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This E-cise uses the floor as a plumb surface. This assists in removing rotation, as well as developing proper joint position.

Name: Sleeper Stretch
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your side in the fetal position
Place your chin onto the bottom shoulder
Place the upper hand onto the wrist of the lower hand
Pull the bottom hand toward the floor while pressing your chin into the bottom shoulder to help stabilize it
Hold as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

This e-cise promotes unilateral scapular stabilization.

Name: Sitting Overhead Extension
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Sit in the middle of a chair with your feet pointed straight ahead, 4-6 inches apart.
Roll your hips forward to place an arch in your low back.
Interlace your fingers together and reach your arms overhead, pressing your hands to the ceiling with palms up.
Look up toward your hands and keep your arms straight, do not bend at the elbow.
Hold as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes bilateral pelvic extension and encourages proper function of the upper body in relation to the lower body.

Name: Sitting Cats & Dogs
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Sit in the middle of a chair with your feet pointed straight ahead, 4-6 inches apart.
Your arms can hang loosely to the sides, or be placed in your lap.
For the CAT position: Roll your hips backward to take the arch out of the back.
For the DOG position: Roll your hips forward to place the arch into your low back.
Slowly move back and forth between the Cat and Dog positions.
Repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes flexion and extension of the entire spine by initiating pelvic flexors and extensors.

Name: Full Bridge
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your knees bent and your feet pointed straight ahead
Place your hands on the ground just above your shoulders with your fingers pointed toward your feet
Bridge your hips and back up off the floor into an arch
Hold this position as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

This exercise forces full extension of the spine and hip.

Name: Shoulder Bridges w/ Strap
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your knees bent, with a strap around your knees/thighs
Keep your arms out to your sides with your palms up
Lift your hips up off the floor as high as they will go and keep constant pressure against the strap at your knees
Remember to keep your feet pointed straight ahead and relax your shoulders.
Repeat as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes proper bilateral extension of pelvis and spine.

Name: Rotator Cuff Series 1
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand with your feet pointed straight ahead, hip width apart, your arms at your side, with your hands in the golfer's grip position
The tips of your fingers should be curled into your palms, with your thumbs extended
Your palms should be facing your thighs with your thumbs pointing toward each other
Slightly squeeze your shoulder blades together and hold throughout the e-cise
Lift your arms straight out to your side up to shoulder level with your thumbs pointing down, then lower your arms to the start position
Repeat as directed on menu

Fun Facts

This series of exercises promotes proper function of the shoulder girdle and its stabilizers

Name: Roller Coasters
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Start in the downward dog position
Your hands and feet are on the floor
Pike at the waist, keeping your knees straight and inline with your hips
Keep your thighs tight
Bend your arms lowering your chest toward the floor
Keep an arch in your back
Skim your chin, then chest, then your stomach on the floor as your roll forward into the elevated cobra position
In the elevated cobra position your elbows should be straight and your hands should be slightly wider than shoulder width apart
Allow your shoulder blades to collapse together
Your hips should now be up off the floor by one to two inches
During the entire motion your knees should never touch the floor
Now pull your hips up into the downward dog position as your stomach, then your chest, then your chin skim the floor in a reverse of the first motion
Repeat as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes thoracic extension under extreme pelvic and lumbar demand

Name: Isolated Hamstring Stretch
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with arms out to sides, palms up, near a doorway with your buttocks as close to the doorway as possible
Put one leg up on the wall, keeping the leg straight
Your other leg should extend through the doorway, supported on a block (or step-stool, etc.)
Relax the leg supported by the block, while the leg on the wall has the thigh tight and the toes pulled back
You should feel a stretch in the back of the leg that is on the wall
As the stretch on your leg decreases you should feel your back settle into the floor
Relax your upper body
Incrementally lower the supported leg as directed on your menu
Switch sides and repeat

Fun Facts

Promotes unilateral hip engagement

Name: Full Sit-Ups
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your knees bent.
Place your feet on the floor (parallel and hip width apart) with someone or something holding your ankles/feet onto the floor as you perform the sit-up.
Interlace your hands behind your head.
Look up and back toward the ceiling.
Keep your elbows back.
Keep your back straight as you sit-up and slowly lower yourself down to the floor without losing the straight back position.
The muscles in the front of your hips will be doing the work to pull you up.
Repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise repositions the pelvis and encourages proper lumbar extension.

Name: Forearm Stretch
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand facing a wall about 6 inches away from the wall, with your feet pointed straight ahead.
Place your palms against the wall with your fingers pointing toward the floor.
You should have about a 90 degree angle in your elbows.
Lean your body slowly closer to the wall without moving your feet.
Your ankles, knees, hips and shoulders should remain aligned at all times.
Your shoulder blades should glide together as you move toward the wall.
Hold this position as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

It is not often that we get wrist extension in daily activity. This E-cise is introducing this position. Remember: "If you don't use it, you lose it."

Name: Flutter Kicks
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back on the floor with both legs straight, hip width apart
Place your hands under your hips (as if you were sitting on them) with your palms down
Lift your upper body and elbows up off the floor about 3 inches
Hold this position throughout the e-cise
Flex your feet, pulling your toes back so they are pointed toward the ceiling
Begin lifting one foot at a time up off the floor at a pretty good rate of speed, kicking your feet up and down
Repeat the kicking motion with your feet flexed as directed on your menu
Now point your toes forward and continue kicking them up and down at a pretty good rate of speed
Repeat the kicking motion with your feet pointed as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

This exercise encourages proper function of the hip flexor muscles while maintaining alignment of the ankle, knee, and hip.

Name: Drape
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Kneel down facing the large block or an ottoman.
Drape your upper body and stomach area over the block/ottoman.
Trail your arms on the side of the block/ottoman and drop your head.
Breathe and hold this position allowing all of your back musculature to react to this new position.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes flexion of the spine.

Name: Diva Pose
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Sit on the floor with both knees bent and pointed in the same direction.
One knee and thigh will be turned inward with the inner thigh and knee on the floor.
This foot should be as far out from the body as possible.
The knee of the internally rotated leg should be touching the bottom foot of the other leg.
The other leg will be turned outward with the outer thigh touching the floor.
Place your hands behind you and use them for stability as needed.
Roll your hips forward to place an arch in your low back.
Be sure to maintain this arch in your back throughout the e-cise.
Try to get both hips onto the floor without losing the correct back position.
Hold as directed on your menu.
Switch sides and repeat.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes extension of the lumbar spine and forces full range of motion at the hip joints.

Name: Doorknob Stretch
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand perpendicular to a doorway.
Your feet should be directly below your hips and pointed straight ahead.
Squat down by rolling your pelvis forward to place a stable arch in your low back.
Hold this position throughout the e-cise.
With the outside arm, reach across your body to grab the door handle or door frame.
Place the inside arm on the small of your low back with the palm facing the ceiling.
Twist your upper body (only) away from the door by pulling the inside elbow away from the door.
Look behind you, over the shoulder of the inside arm to extend the twist.  Keep your knees in their starting position, parallel and pointed forward, hip width apart.
Hold as directed on your menu, then switch sides.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes trunk rotation with pelvic stabilization.

Name: Hooklying Arm Glides
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor pointed straight ahead
Keeping your arms on the floor, raise both arms up until they are directly out from the shoulder
Bend your elbows to create a 90 degree angle, with the back of your hands on the floor
Glide both arms up until they are straight up from the shoulder and hold there for five seconds
Be careful not to over reach and twist your torso
Return both arms to the bent arm position
Repeat as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

Promotes bilateral scapular rotation

Name: Lunges
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand with both feet pointed straight ahead, and your arms down at your sides.
Hold weights in your hands if directed to do so on your menu.
Take a large stride forward.
Stay balanced as you allow your body to lunge forward and down.
Step far enough forward so that when you lunge forward your lunging knee is directly over your foot, and the other knee can just barely touch the floor.
Lift yourself back to the beginning position.
Feet together, hip width apart, pointed straight ahead.
Switch legs and repeat.
Continue alternating legs and repeat as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise strengthens all the muscles of the pelvic girdle.

Name: Modified Floor Block
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your stomach with your forehead on the floor
Your feet should be pigeon-toed and your buttocks relaxed
Elevate your bent arms 4 to 9 inches by placing your forearms and elbows on blocks
Breathe and relax your upper body
Hold as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes passive pelvic and lumbar extension by changing the position of the upper back

Name: Wall Frog w/ Pull-over
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your hips up against the wall
Place the bottom of your feet together against the wall and let your hips and knees drop out to the side
The sides of your feet should now be against the wall
Keep your tailbone in contact with the floor, scoot out from the wall if your tailbone comes off the floor
Reach arms straight up above your chest, elbows locked and hands clasped together
Now lower your hands down to the floor above your head
Do not contract your abdominal muscles, keep the stomach and lower back relaxed
Do not let your arms bend at the elbow, keep them straight throughout the e-cise
If you are unable to lower your hands all the way to the floor behind you, go only as low as you are able while maintaining the straight arm position
Return to the starting position and repeat as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

The movement of this exercise contributes to pelvic extension through lateral hip demand

Name: Wall Frog w/ Pull-over Presses
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your hips up against a wall
Place the bottom of your feet together against the wall and let your hips and knees drop out to the side
The sides of your feet should now be against the wall
Keep your tailbone in contact with the floor, scoot out from the wall if your tailbone comes off the floor
Place a small pillow/block above your head
Interlace your fingers and extend your arms straight overhead to rest on the pillow/block
Make sure to keep your palms together and your elbows locked
Use your shoulders (not your arms) to press down and release on the block
Repeat as directed on your menu

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes spinal extensor demand to assist in pelvic extension through lateral hip demand

Name: Wall Frog
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your hips up against the wall.
Place the bottom of your feet together against the wall and let your hips and knees drop out to the side.
The sides of your feet should now be against the wall.
Keep your tailbone in contact with the floor, scoot out from the wall if your tailbone comes off the floor.
Place your arms out to the side at 45 degrees with palms up.
Keep your stomach, neck and back relaxed.
Hold as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes pelvic extension through lateral hip demand.

Name: Standing Wall Clock
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Stand facing a wall with toes pointed inward (pigeon-toed) and your forehead against the wall.
Position 1: Raise your arms directly overhead.
Relax your stomach to allow your hips to tilt forward.
Make a golfer's grip with your hands (fingers curled toward the palm, thumbs out).
Rotate your thumbs/arms outward from the shoulder (moving the back of your hand toward the wall).
Hold for 1 minute (or as directed on your menu).
Position 2: Move your hands down to 45 degrees.
Repeat the outward rotation with your arms from the shoulders.
Hold for 1 minute (or as directed on your menu).
Position 3: Move your arms down to 90 degrees (straight out from your shoulders, parallel to floor).
Repeat the outward rotation with your arms from the shoulders.
Hold for 1 minute (or as directed on your menu).
Repeat the entire sequence as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes extension throughout the body with proper alignment of the load joints.

Name: Upper Spinal Floor Twist
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on one side in the fetal position with your arms straight out from your shoulders in front of you.
Stack your knees one directly atop the other, where they should remain throughout the e-cise.
Open the top arm, lifting it up and over your body to the other side letting it rest to the floor or as close to the floor as you are able.
Move your head to look in the same direction as that arm.
Do NOT let your knees come apart while moving the arm to the other side.
Use your bottom hand to hold your knees together and keep them stacked on top of one another.
Remember to breathe.
Allow your body to open up.
Hold as directed on your menu.
Switch sides and repeat.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes upper torso rotation to engage the pelvic girdle stabilization response.

Name: Towel Settle w/ Pillow
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor pointed straight at hip width.
Place a pillow between your knees and apply constant, gentle pressure.
Place a roll/towel under your low back (behind your belly-button) and under your neck.
Relax arms at 45 degrees from sides with palms facing up and breathe through the stomach/diaphragm.
Allow your body to settle on the rolls.
Stay in this position as directed on your menu.

Fun Facts

This exercise re-educates the proper position of the spinal curves.

Name: Supine Groin Stretch w/ Towels
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back in the supine groin position with one leg straight out on the floor and the other leg on a block or a chair with the knee bent at 90 degrees.
Place a 2-3 inch roll behind your neck and low back.
It's very important that the straight leg is propped vertically by something heavy so the toes point to the ceiling.
Place your arms on the floor, 45 degrees from your body with the palms facing up. Relax your legs and upper body.
Ask your Therapist about the thigh test.
Switch legs and repeat.

Fun Facts

Sitting for extended periods throughout the day causes a number of negative side effects to our posture, including loss of our designed spinal curves and short and tight groin muscles.  This E-cise helps to reverse the negative effects caused from sitting all day.  The Towels help to re-educate our spine of it's natural "S" curve.

Name: Supine Groin Progressive-Modified
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on the floor with one leg up over a block or chair, bent to 90 degrees.
Your arms should be out to your sides at 45 degrees with palms facing up.
Place your foot on the stairs or step stool(keep toes pointing to the ceiling) starting at the step advised by your therapist or at the level where an arch begins to form in your lower back.
Relax your upper body and let your body adjust at each new level.
Hold as directed on your menu or until your back is flat on the floor.
Pay more attention to the flattening of your back than the specified time.
Lower your foot one step on the stairs and again hold.
Continue this until your leg is extended straight out on the lowest step, unless directed to do otherwise by your therapist.
Switch legs and repeat the entire sequence.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes an elongation of the hip and groin muscles. The effect of this action will create a chain reaction of muscular response in the upper body.

Name: Supine Calf & Hamstring Stretch
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your knees bent
Position 1: Take a strap or belt and loop it under the ball of one foot
Firmly holding the ends of the strap, straighten that leg and PULL YOUR TOES DOWN toward your hips to feel a stretch in the calf or back of your lower leg
The straight leg should be lined up with the bent knee.
Keep your thigh/quad tight and toes pulled back
Your upper body should be relaxed throughout the e-cise, shoulders back on the floor
Position 2: Loop the strap under the heel of the straight leg
Pull back on the strap so your leg points towards the ceiling, you will feel a stretch in the back of your thigh.  Keep your thigh tight and TOES RELAXED.
Do NOT let the hip lift up off the floor
Hold as directed on your menu (30 to 60 seconds)
Switch legs and repeat both positions

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes unilateral lumbar engagement with emphasis on using pelvic flexors.

Name: Static Wall w/ Lumbar Roll
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your legs pointed straight up and against the wall, upper body relaxed with arms out to sides at about 45 degrees and palms facing up
Place a support roll or towel at the small of your lower back, right behind your belly button
Your hips should rest to the floor around the roll. If they do not hit the floor, move away from the wall as far as needed until they do
In this position tighten your thighs and pull your toes down and back toward you
Your feet must be pointed straight for your hips to properly do the needed work to stabilize your spine
Hold as directed on your menu
Be sure to keep your toes pulled back and thighs tight and upper body relaxed

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes thoracic extension while limiting the rotation ability of the lower load joints with emphasis on increased mid thoracic extension

Name: Static Wall Femur Rotations
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™

Lie on your back with your legs pointed straight up and against the wall, upper body relaxed with arms out to sides at about 45 degrees and palms facing up.
When getting into this position, get your hips as close as possible to the wall, so once you are in position your butt is also as close as possible to the wall.
If your hips are lifting off the floor then scoot your butt back until you are able to place your hips flat. As you get more functional, your hips will sit closer to the wall and be flat on the floor at the same time.
In this position tighten your thighs and pull your toes down and back toward you.
Your feet must be pointed straight for your hips to properly do the needed work to stabilize your spine.
Rotate your legs/feet in and out using the hips to initiate the movement.
Be sure to keep your toes pulled back and your thighs tight.
Be sure your upper body remains relaxed.
Repeat as directed on your menu.
There are 3 positions for this e-cise: (1) Legs straight up, the beginning position (2) out about 25 degrees (3) out further to about 50 degrees.

Fun Facts

This exercise promotes thoracic extension while limiting the rotation ability of the lower load joints with an emphasis on hip stability.

Name: Static Extension
Description:
How to Perform this E-cise™
